slides.title: A small calf move that *steadies your blood sugar*.
slides.sub: It's called the soleus pushup. You can do it sitting down, without breaking a sweat.

slides.title: Meet the soleus muscle
slides.body: Deep in your calf sits the soleus. It is small, but it is built for *hours of low effort* and rarely tires.

slides.title: The seated soleus pushup
slides.body: Sit with feet flat, then slowly raise and lower your heels. The toes stay down and the calf does the work, *quietly and steadily*.

slides.title: It burns local fuel
slides.body: That gentle pumping keeps the soleus *oxidizing blood glucose* for hours, drawing sugar from the blood even while you sit.

slides.title: Calmer post-meal spikes
slides.body: Research suggests the soleus pushup *can blunt the glucose rise* after meals. A small habit at your desk, no gym required.

slides.title: Pair it with sitting
slides.body: Tie it to the time you already sit: meetings, meals, screen time. Steady reps *may help* keep energy even through the afternoon.

slides.title: Turn small moves into *real progress*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your movement, food, sleep and steps, then rolls them into one daily Health Score so quiet habits like this actually add up.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
There's a tiny calf muscle that can help steady your blood sugar while you sit. It's called the soleus pushup. 🌿

Sit with feet flat, slowly raise and lower your heels, and let the soleus quietly burn glucose for hours. It's a desk-friendly habit that can blunt post-meal spikes. Save this for your next long sitting day. 🔖

Vibe Health turns small habits like this into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: The Seated Soleus Pushup That Steadies Blood Sugar
